summary: Phase 2 (Parent Dashboard + Community Freedom Journal + AI moderation) verified end-to-end on https://azaadi-stories.preview.emergentagent.com. Backend: 17/17 new pytest cases PASSED + 15/15 Phase-1 regression PASSED (32/32 = 100%). Covered: parent signup with role='parent', link-child (success/404/parent-target rejection), GET /parent/children with full kid progress, set-goal with clamping (0→1, 99→10) + persistence, role guards (kid→parent endpoints=403, parent→/journal=403), journal create (linked-kid→pending, solo-kid→approved, PII→AI-rejected), feed shows only approved, /journal/mine shows all statuses, reactions toggle (🔥/💡/🌟) + invalid emoji 400, pending-posts scoped to own children, parent moderate approve/reject + cross-parent moderation 403, length validation. Frontend (390x844 mobile): role toggle hides age/avatar/language for parent, parent signup auto-redirects to /parent, Parent Hub header shown with link-child input + Children/Review tabs, kid card with XP/Stories/Quizzes/Badges + goal chips 1/2/3/5 (functional), Review tab lists pending post with Approve/Reject buttons, Approve removes from review and post appears in feed, AI moderation rejection with reason populated, reactions API increments counts. Found and fixed one minor router warning ('parent' route name → 'parent/index').

issues: RN-web warnings (cosmetic, pre-existing): 'shadow*' style props deprecated, props.pointerEvents deprecated

action_items: Optional cosmetic: replace shadow*/pointerEvents prop usages with boxShadow/style.pointerEvents to silence RN-web deprecation warnings.

Note: AsyncStorage v3 stores under IndexedDB on web (not localStorage); this works correctly via AsyncStorage API but means external scripts can't shortcut auth via localStorage. Not a bug — informational only.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 677 lines with both Phase-1 and Phase-2 routes — consider splitting into modules (auth, stories, journal, parent) for maintainability before next phase.
_moderate_post falls back to UNSAFE on LLM exception (good — fail-closed for kids' safety).
Reactions stored as full user-id arrays inside post doc — fine at MVP scale; add an index on (status, approved_at) and (author_id, status) before scale.
All MongoDB queries correctly project out _id, _serialize_post strips _id and reaction user-id arrays before returning to client (no PII leak).
Parent endpoints all gate via require_parent / explicit parent_id check — verified via 403 tests.
updated_files: /app/frontend/app/_layout.tsx (fixed Stack.Screen name 'parent' → 'parent/index' to remove router warning)

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested Phase-2 flows (auth role toggle, parent signup→/parent, link-child, kid card render, goal set, Review tab, approve, AI moderation rejection, reactions)

seed_data_creation: Per-test ephemeral users created via /api/auth/signup with prefix test_/e2e_ + timestamps. testkid pre-existing seed kept intact. No production data touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable regression suites: /app/backend/tests/test_azaadi_api.py (Phase-1, 15 tests) + /app/backend/tests/test_phase2_parent_journal.py (Phase-2, 17 tests). Run with: EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=<preview-url> pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. UI tip: AsyncStorage v3 on web does NOT use localStorage, so swapping users via JS injection won't work — instead use the Logout button (RN Alert renders as window.confirm on web; register dialog handler with page.once('dialog', d.accept()) BEFORE the click) or perform parallel auth via API + UI for cross-role tests. testIDs catalogue: auth (auth-title, tab-signup/login, role-kid/parent, input-username/password, age-{6|8|10|12|14}, avatar-{emoji}, lang-en/hi, submit-btn); parent (parent-tab-children/review, link-input, link-btn, kid-{id}, goal-{id}-{1|2|3|5}, review-{postId}, approve-{id}, reject-{id}, parent-logout); journal (new-post-btn, journal-tab-feed/mine, post-{id}, react-{id}-{🔥|💡|🌟}); journal/new (post-input, post-submit, story-pick-{id}, np-back).
rca_of_issue: Minor router warning fixed: app/_layout.tsx had <Stack.Screen name='parent' /> but the file system route is 'parent/index' (no _layout under parent/ folder). Expo Router emitted 'No route named parent exists in nested children' on every navigation. Renamed to 'parent/index' — warning gone, behaviour unchanged.
